nodes.label: AI as research orientation tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or unhelpful for research
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI to do research and ask it questions to help orient my own internal research

nodes.label: AI for quick consultation vs ongoing collaboration
nodes.grounding_span: More the second one, I might ask a follow-up question or two but usually it's just general information

nodes.label: Personal touch in client communication
nodes.grounding_span: I find that a personal touch is needed and that the responses might be too robotic if I were to use AI to write them

nodes.label: AI for repetitive task automation
nodes.grounding_span: My company hasn't implemented AI to do any of the repetitive work tasks and that would be useful to free up some time

nodes.label: AI usage limits and tool switching
nodes.grounding_span: when I hit the use limit on chatgpt and it requested payment, then I had to move to gemini

nodes.label: Human judgment vs AI in high-stakes roles
nodes.grounding_span: there was too much human judgment required, such as unforeseen circumstances and nuance

nodes.label: AI as information aggregator and efficiency advisor
nodes.grounding_span: it would be good if they used them to enhance situational awareness by providing data in real-time to assist
